title: Orchestrator should detect and clean up zombie worker sessions

comments.author: micahmartin

title: Update orch header/footer format — DRY-RUN/LIVE-RUN started/completed at timestamp
description:
### Summary

Update the orchestrator tick output to use new header/footer format with started/completed timestamps.

### Current format

Header only, no footer:
```
── DRY-RUN ── 2026-03-08 14:30:00
── CONFIRMED ── 2026-03-08 14:30:00
```

### New format

Header:
```
-- DRY-RUN started at 2026-03-08 14:30:00 --
-- LIVE-RUN started at 2026-03-08 14:30:00 --
```

Footer:
```
-- DRY-RUN completed at 2026-03-08 14:30:05 --
-- LIVE-RUN completed at 2026-03-08 14:30:05 --
```

### Changes

- `orch_runner_io.clj` — Update header println to new format, rename CONFIRMED to LIVE-RUN, add footer println at end of `run-orch!`
- Feature scenarios already updated in `orch_output.feature`

### Acceptance criteria

- `bb spec` — all green
- `bb features` — all green
- Header/footer scenarios pass (remove @wip)
- Running `bb braids orch` shows `-- DRY-RUN started at ...` and `-- DRY-RUN completed at ...`
- Running `bb braids orch --confirmed` shows `-- LIVE-RUN started at ...` and `-- LIVE-RUN completed at ...`

Run orchestrator in persistent session with aggressive compaction
description:
Currently the orchestrator runs in isolated sessions (sessionTarget: isolated), reloading all context every 5 minutes. This is expensive and contributes to timeouts.

Proposed approach:
1. Switch cron sessionTarget to a persistent session key (e.g. 'braids:orchestrator')
2. Configure aggressive compaction for that session so the transcript stays small after each run
3. Orchestrator.md stays in system prompt context — no need to re-read each tick
4. Document how to configure this in SKILL.md

The session should compact after each run summarizing what was spawned/idled. Combined with braids-3m6 (3-tool-call flow), each run becomes very lightweight.

Config change needed in openclaw.json or via cron edit --session-key braids:orchestrator

title: Pass session labels to orch-run CLI for concurrency-aware spawn decisions
description:
Currently braids orch-run ignores active workers, defaulting worker counts to 0. The orchestrator agent must call sessions_list, parse labels, check bead status per session (expensive), and do its own concurrency filtering — burning many tokens and causing timeouts.

Fix: Accept active session labels as CLI input so the CLI handles concurrency checking in pure Clojure.

Interface (proposed):
  braids orch-run --sessions 'project:zaap:abc project:braids:xyz'

The CLI then:
1. Parses the label list to extract slug and bead-id per session
2. Checks bead status for each (bd show) to detect zombies (closed bead = zombie)
3. Counts healthy workers per project
4. Filters spawn list to respect max-workers per project

Updated orchestrator flow becomes just 3 tool calls:
1. sessions_list → extract project: labels
2. braids orch-run --sessions <labels> → filtered spawn list
3. sessions_spawn for each entry

Also update orchestrator.md to reflect the new simplified flow.

2. Add --confirm flag: sets dry-run=false to perform actual actions
3. Update logging: clearly indicate 'DRY-RUN' vs 'CONFIRMED' mode in all log messages
4. Update help text: document the new default behavior and --confirm flag
5. Update orchestrator.md if needed to reflect the new CLI behavior

Benefits:
- Safety: No accidental worker spawning from casual 'braids orch' commands
- Clarity: Explicit confirmation required for side effects
- Backward compatibility: --dry-run flag still works (but is now default)
- Observability: Logs clearly show execution mode

title: Refine disable logic: blocked beads should not keep orchestrator alive
description:
Current behavior after braids-914: orchestrator stays alive if any active iteration has open (non-closed) beads. But BLOCKED beads are effectively stuck — they need human intervention, not more orchestrator polling.

Correct logic:
- Only count beads that are actually workable (open/in-progress but NOT blocked) toward 'keep alive'
- If all remaining open beads are blocked, treat same as no ready beads → disable_cron: true
- This mirrors bd ready behavior: blocked beads don't show as ready

Implementation: in orch.clj, when checking open-beads, filter out beads with status 'blocked'. Only non-blocked open beads should keep the cron alive.

title: Implement orch_spawning.feature — step patterns, generator, and harness
description:
### Summary

Implement the step patterns, generator registry entries, and harness functions needed to make the rewritten `features/orch_spawning.feature` scenarios executable.

### New step patterns needed (beyond what orch_output adds)

1. `the spawns should include` (with table) — asserts spawn entries contain expected attributes. Table columns are flexible — only specified columns are checked.
2. `the spawns should not include bead "X"` — asserts bead ID not in any spawn entry
3. `the spawn for "X" should not have key "Y"` — asserts a key is absent from a specific spawn
4. `spawn N should be for project "X"` — asserts spawn ordering by index

### Shared step patterns (from orch_output, reused here)

- `configured projects:` (with table) — upsert project config
- `project "X" has beads:` (with table) — set beads per project
- `the orchestrator ticks` — already exists
- `the action should be "X"` — already exists
- `the idle reason should be "X"` — already exists

### Harness functions needed

- `spawn-includes? [expected-row]` — check spawn list contains entry matching all columns
- `spawn-excludes-bead? [bead-id]` — check no spawn has this bead
- `spawn-missing-key? [bead-id key]` — check specific spawn doesn't have key
- `spawn-at [n]` — get nth spawn entry (1-indexed)

### Acceptance criteria

- `bb spec` — all green
- `bb features` — all orch_spawning scenarios pass (remove @wip tags)
- All 11 orch_spawning scenarios executable

title: Implement orch_spawning feature specs
description:
## Add orch_spawning domain to typed-IR pipeline — all 7 scenarios green

This is the SECOND bead in a 3-bead sequence. braids-bmte built the typed-IR infrastructure and zombie_detection domain. This bead adds orch_spawning patterns.

### What to do

Add parser patterns, generator dispatchers, and harness helpers for the orch_spawning feature so that `bb test:features` produces green specs for all 7 orch_spawning scenarios.

### Orch spawning step patterns to implement

**GIVEN:**
- `a project "X" with max-workers N` → `{:type :project-config :slug X :max-workers N}`
  NOTE: :project-config type already exists from bmte (for worker-timeout). Extend it or merge.
- `project "X" has an active iteration "N"` → `{:type :active-iteration :slug X :iteration N}`
- `project "X" has no active iteration` → `{:type :no-active-iteration :slug X}`
- `project "X" has N ready beads` → `{:type :ready-beads :slug X :count N}`
- `project "X" has 1 ready bead with id "Y"` → `{:type :ready-bead-with-id :slug X :bead-id Y}`
- `project "X" has N active workers` → `{:type :active-workers :slug X :count N}`

**WHEN:**
- `the orchestrator ticks` → `{:type :orch-tick}`
- `the orchestrator ticks for project "X" only` → `{:type :orch-tick-project :slug X}`

**THEN:**
- `the action should be "X"` → `{:type :assert-action :expected X}`
- `N workers should be spawned` → `{:type :assert-spawn-count :count N}`
- `the idle reason should be "X"` → `{:type :assert-idle-reason :expected X}`
- `the spawn label should be "X"` → `{:type :assert-spawn-label :expected X}`

### Harness additions
The harness needs orch-tick helpers:
- `add-project-to-registry` — add project to registry with active status
- `set-active-iteration` — set iteration for a project
- `set-ready-beads` — set ready bead list for a project
- `set-active-workers` — set worker count for a project
- `tick!` — call `orch/tick` with accumulated state, store result
- `result-action`, `result-spawn-count`, `result-idle-reason`, `result-spawn-label` — accessors on tick result

### Acceptance criteria
- `bb test:features` — all 7 orch_spawning scenarios GREEN + all 5 zombie_detection scenarios still GREEN
- `bb test` still passes
- Feature files IMMUTABLE

title: Label-based agent routing — orchestrator reads bead labels to select worker agent (e.g. 'spec' label routes to prowl)
description:
## Goal
The orchestrator checks bead labels to determine which agent to spawn for a given bead, instead of always using the project default (:worker-agent).

## Config
In .braids/config.edn, add :agent-overrides map:
{:worker-agent "scrapper"
 :agent-overrides {"spec" "prowl"}}

## Behavior
- If bead has label matching a key in :agent-overrides → use that agent
- If no label match → use :worker-agent (default)
- Label matching is case-insensitive

## Acceptance
- Bead with label 'spec' in a project with prowl override → spawns prowl worker
- Bead without label → spawns default worker-agent

title: Refine orchestrator disable logic: only stay alive if active iterations have open beads
description:
Current behavior (braids-q6p fix): disable_cron:false whenever reason is 'no-ready-beads', assuming active iterations exist. Too broad — keeps cron alive even when active iterations have zero open beads (e.g. empty iteration, all beads closed/blocked).

Correct logic:
- disable_cron: true when no active iterations exist
- disable_cron: true when all active iterations have no open (non-closed) beads  
- disable_cron: false when at least one active iteration has open beads

Implementation: in orch.clj, after collecting active projects/iterations, check bd list (or bd ready --include-blocked) per project to see if any open beads exist. If none across all projects, set disable_cron: true regardless of idle reason.

This handles the edge case where an iteration is active but empty or fully blocked with no hope of becoming ready.

title: Orchestrator: fire all spawns without waiting, exit immediately after
description:
The orchestrator is timing out because it spawns workers sequentially and waits for each sessions_spawn tool call to complete before firing the next one. Even though sessions_spawn is fire-and-forget, the tool call itself has overhead.

Fix orchestrator.md to make it explicit:
1. Fire ALL sessions_spawn calls back-to-back without waiting between them
2. Do NOT wait for any spawn to complete or confirm
3. After all spawns are fired (and zombies killed), exit IMMEDIATELY
4. The orchestrator's job is done the moment it fires the spawns — it should not linger

Also: reduce the cron timeout from 600s back to 120s now that the tick is fast (CLI runs in <5s) and the agent only needs to fire spawns. If it takes more than 2 minutes something is wrong.

- Points to ~/.openclaw/skills/projects/references/worker.md as the worker convention doc
- Also includes the public URL (clawhub.com or GitHub) for worker.md so agents on machines without OpenClaw installed can still follow conventions
- Contains project-specific context (bd quick ref, git push requirements) in minimal form
- Serves as THE single boot file any agent reads when entering a project

The skill should generate this AGENTS.md during project creation (bd init or project setup). Orchestrator spawn message can be simplified since AGENTS.md handles onboarding.

Eliminates duplication between bd AGENTS.md and worker.md. Any entry point — orchestrator, manual, external — gets full guidance.

title: Implement worker_session_tracking feature specs
description:
## Add worker_session_tracking domain to typed-IR pipeline — all 4 scenarios green

This is the THIRD and final bead in a 3-bead sequence. This bead adds worker_session_tracking patterns.

### What to do

Add parser patterns, generator dispatchers, and harness helpers for the worker_session_tracking feature so that `bb test:features` produces green specs for all 4 scenarios.

### Session tracking step patterns to implement

**GIVEN:**
- `a bead with id "X"` → `{:type :bead :bead-id X}`
- `another bead with id "X"` → `{:type :bead :bead-id X}`
- `a session ID "X"` → `{:type :session-id-literal :session-id X}`

**WHEN:**
- `generating the session ID` → `{:type :generate-session-id}`
- `generating the session ID twice` → `{:type :generate-session-id-twice}`
- `generating session IDs for both` → `{:type :generate-session-ids-both}`
- `parsing the session ID` → `{:type :parse-session-id}`

**THEN:**
- `the session ID should be "X"` → `{:type :assert-session-id :expected X}`
- `both session IDs should be identical` → `{:type :assert-ids-identical}`
- `the session IDs should be different` → `{:type :assert-ids-different}`
- `the extracted bead ID should be "X"` → `{:type :assert-bead-id :expected X}`

### Harness additions
- `set-bead-id` / `set-bead-ids` — store bead ID(s) for the current scenario
- `set-session-id-literal` — store a literal session ID string
- `generate-session-id!` — call `orch/worker-session-id`, store result
- `generate-session-id-twice!` — call it twice, store both results
- `generate-session-ids-both!` — call for both bead IDs, store results
- `parse-session-id!` — call `orch/parse-worker-session-id`, store result
- Assertion accessors: `session-id-result`, `session-ids-identical?`, `session-ids-different?`, `parsed-bead-id`

### Acceptance criteria
- `bb test:features` — all 4 session tracking + 7 orch_spawning + 5 zombie_detection = 16 scenarios GREEN
- `bb test` still passes
- Feature files IMMUTABLE

title: Implement zombie_detection feature specs
description:
## Build typed-IR feature test infrastructure + zombie_detection green

This is the FIRST bead in a 3-bead sequence. It builds the infrastructure for generating executable feature specs (not pending stubs), using zombie_detection as the proving ground.

### Architecture (empire-2025 pattern)

The pipeline is: .feature → parser → typed IR (.edn) → generator → executable speclj specs

Currently the parser stores steps as raw strings and the generator emits `(pending)`. This bead evolves both:

1. **Enhanced parser** (`src/braids/gherkin.clj`): Pattern-match each Given/When/Then step text into a typed IR node map with `:type` and extracted data. Example:
   - `"a project \"proj\" with worker-timeout 3600"` → `{:type :project-config :slug "proj" :worker-timeout 3600}`
   - `"session \"s1\" has status \"running\" and age 100 seconds"` → `{:type :session-status :session-id "s1" :status "running" :age-seconds 100}`
   - `"checking for zombies"` → `{:type :check-zombies}`
   - `"session \"s1\" should be a zombie with reason \"bead-closed\""` → `{:type :assert-zombie :session-id "s1" :reason "bead-closed"}`

   Steps that do not match any pattern become `{:type :unrecognized :text "..."}`.

2. **Enhanced generator** (`src/braids/gherkin_generator.clj`): Dispatch on IR `:type` to emit executable Clojure code strings (not pending). Each Given emits setup code, each When emits action code, each Then emits assertion code. The generated spec calls a test harness.

3. **Test harness** (`src/braids/features/harness.clj`): Provides helper functions the generated code calls. For zombie_detection:
   - State-building helpers: `add-project-config`, `add-session`, `set-session-status`, `set-bead-status`
   - Action helpers: `check-zombies!` (calls `orch/detect-zombies` with accumulated state)
   - The harness manages mutable test state via atoms, reset before each scenario

4. **Generated specs**: `bb test:features` produces specs like:
   ```clojure
   (it "Detect zombie when bead is closed but session still running"
     (h/reset!)
     (h/add-project-config "proj" {:worker-timeout 3600})
     (h/add-session "s1" {:label "project:proj:proj-abc"})
     (h/set-session-status "s1" "running" 100)
     (h/set-bead-status "proj-abc" "closed")
     (h/check-zombies!)
     (should (h/zombie? "s1"))
     (should= "bead-closed" (h/zombie-reason "s1")))
   ```

### Zombie detection step patterns to implement

**GIVEN:**
- `a project "X" with worker-timeout N` → `{:type :project-config :slug X :worker-timeout N}`
- `a session "X" with label "Y"` → `{:type :session :session-id X :label Y}`
- `session "X" has status "Y" and age N seconds` → `{:type :session-status :session-id X :status Y :age-seconds N}`
- `bead "X" has status "Y"` → `{:type :bead-status :bead-id X :status Y}`
- `bead "X" has no recorded status` → `{:type :bead-no-status :bead-id X}`

**WHEN:**
- `checking for zombies` → `{:type :check-zombies}`

**THEN:**
- `session "X" should be a zombie with reason "Y"` → `{:type :assert-zombie :session-id X :reason Y}`
- `no zombies should be detected` → `{:type :assert-no-zombies}`

### Acceptance criteria
- `bb test:features` runs and all 5 zombie_detection scenarios are GREEN (not pending)
- `bb test` still passes (unit tests unbroken)
- Parser produces typed IR nodes (not raw strings) for zombie_detection steps
- Generator produces executable code (not pending) for zombie_detection specs
- Unrecognized steps (from other features) produce `{:type :unrecognized}` and generate `(pending)` — this is fine, the other features will be green in subsequent beads
- Test harness is in `src/braids/features/harness.clj`
- Feature files are IMMUTABLE — do not modify .feature files

### Files to create/modify
- `src/braids/gherkin.clj` — enhance parser to produce typed IR
- `src/braids/gherkin_generator.clj` — enhance generator to dispatch on types
- `src/braids/features/harness.clj` — NEW: test harness
- `spec/braids/gherkin_spec.clj` — update parser tests for typed IR
- `spec/braids/gherkin_generator_spec.clj` — update generator tests for code output

### Reference
Study `/Users/micahmartin/Projects/empire-2025/src/empire/acceptance/parser.cljc` and `generator.cljc` for the pattern. The generated code should call harness functions, not inline raw orch calls.

title: Move dev-only deps to per-task :extra-deps in bb.edn
description:
### Problem

All deps (speclj, clj-mutate, tools.reader) are declared at the top level of bb.edn. Running any task — including \`bb braids\` (the production CLI) — triggers babashka to resolve all dependencies. On machines without the \`clojure\` CLI or network access, this fails with a babashka.process exception even though the braids CLI doesn't need speclj or clj-mutate.

### Fix

Move dev-only deps to \`:extra-deps\` on the tasks that need them:

- \`speclj/speclj\` → \`:extra-deps\` on \`spec\`, \`features\`, and \`test:all\` tasks
- \`io.github.slagyr/clj-mutate\` → \`:extra-deps\` on \`mutate\` task
- \`org.clojure/tools.reader\` — check if this is needed by braids core or only by dev tasks

The \`braids\` task should have zero deps beyond what babashka provides built-in plus what's on the \`:paths\`.

### Acceptance criteria

- \`bb braids --help\` works without clojure CLI installed (no dep resolution needed)
- \`bb spec\` still works (resolves speclj via extra-deps)
- \`bb features\` still works
- \`bb mutate\` still works

    juw  open

description:
## Problem
orch_runner.clj includes --deliver --reply-channel discord --reply-to <channel> on every worker spawn. This causes two critical bugs:

1. Every worker response is auto-delivered to the Discord channel (spam)
2. The worker session gets associated with the Discord channel context, so agents apply group-chat silence rules and respond NO_REPLY to the task message — workers never start

## Fix
Remove --deliver, --reply-channel, and --reply-to from base-args in build-worker-args.
Workers should only reach Discord via explicit message tool calls for defined notification events (bead-start, bead-complete, blocker, question, etc.) — not via automatic delivery.

## Acceptance
- Workers spawn without --deliver flag
- No automatic channel output from workers
- Workers respond to task messages (no longer NO_REPLY)
- Notification events still work via explicit message calls

title: Fix gherkin runner core — load-step-defs and match-step
description:
Three critical bugs prevent the gherkin runner from working:

1. load-step-defs: load-file returns a Var (from def), not the map value. Fix: step def files should return the map as the last expression (remove def wrapper), OR the runner should dereference the Var.

2. match-step: step def keys are strings with regex syntax (e.g. "Given (\\d+) ready beads") but match-step only checks java.util.regex.Pattern instances. Following cucumber convention, the runner should auto-compile string keys to Pattern at load time, then extract capture groups and pass them as arguments to the step function.

3. run-feature uses lazy for with side effects. Wrap in doall.

4. run-features calls System/exit — remove it, return exit code instead.

Convention: step def keys are strings with regex capture groups (following cucumber-jvm/cucumber-js). The runner compiles them to Patterns. Capture groups are passed as args to step fns.

title: Orchestrator: use --session-id when spawning workers so worker sessions are trackable across cron ticks
description:
## Problem
The orchestrator spawns workers via `openclaw agent --agent <agent> --message <task>` but doesn't set a session ID. Each cron tick loads sessions fresh and can't find the previously spawned worker session — so workers=0 every tick and the same bead gets re-spawned indefinitely.

## Fix
Generate a deterministic session ID per bead (e.g. `braids-<bead-id>-worker`) and pass it via `--session-id` when invoking `openclaw agent`. On subsequent ticks, the orchestrator checks for that session ID to determine if a worker is already running for that bead.

## Acceptance
- Orchestrator spawns worker with `--session-id braids-<bead-id>-worker`
- Next tick correctly detects the running worker session and does not re-spawn
- Zombie detection still works (session ended = no longer active)

title: Gherkin parser — .feature to .edn IR
description:
Build a gherkin parser that converts .feature files to .edn intermediate representation. Rewrite src/braids/gherkin.clj.

Input: .feature files in spec/features/
Output: .edn files in spec/features/edn/

IR format:
{:source "orch_spawning.feature"
 :feature "Orchestrator spawning behavior"
 :background {:givens ["a project ..." "project ..."]}
 :scenarios [{:scenario "Spawn workers when..."
              :givens ["project alpha has 3 ready beads"]
              :whens ["the orchestrator ticks"]
              :thens ["the action should be spawn" "2 workers should be spawned"]}]}

Rules:
- Background givens are NOT merged into scenarios — they stay separate so the generator can decide how to handle them
- Given/When/Then/And/But keywords are stripped — just the text
- And/But steps are appended to the previous phase (Given And -> both in givens)
- @wip tagged scenarios get :wip true in the IR
- Feature description text (non-scenario prose) is preserved in :description

The gherkin code stays in src/ — we just decouple it from the default test run.

title: Fix orchestrator self-disable: stay enabled while active iterations exist
description:
The orchestrator currently disables itself whenever there are no ready beads (bd ready returns empty). This is too aggressive — it should only disable when there are no active iterations across all projects.

The correct logic:
- Disable: no active iterations exist (nothing could ever become ready)
- Stay enabled: active iterations exist, even if no beads are ready right now (beads may become unblocked)

The orch-tick/orch-run CLI output includes the disable_cron flag. This flag should only be true when there are truly no active iterations, not just when no beads are currently ready.

Fix in src/braids/orch.clj — the idle reason 'no-ready-beads' should NOT trigger disable_cron. Only 'no-active-iterations' should.

title: Implement zombie_detection WIP scenarios — cleanup and multi-project detection
description:
### Summary

Implement the 2 @wip scenarios in zombie_detection.feature.

### Scenarios

1. **Zombie cleanup kills sessions and reports results** — After zombies are detected, running cleanup should kill the zombie sessions and produce a report listing each killed session and its reason.

2. **Zombie detection across multiple projects** — Zombies are detected correctly when sessions span multiple projects with different worker-timeout configurations.

### New step patterns needed

1. \`zombies have been detected\` — set up state where zombies have already been detected
2. \`cleaning up zombies\` — trigger zombie cleanup action
3. \`the zombie sessions should be killed\` — assert sessions were killed
4. \`a cleanup report should list each killed session and its reason\` — assert report contents

### Notes

Scenario 2 mostly reuses existing step patterns (already has project setup, session setup, zombie checking, and assertion patterns). The multi-project aspect just verifies that different worker-timeout values are applied per project.

### Acceptance criteria

- \`bb spec\` — all green
- \`bb features\` — both zombie_detection WIP scenarios pass (remove @wip)

It must NOT edit project files (SKILL.md, worker.md, orchestrator.md, PROJECT.md, CONTRACTS.md, etc.) directly. All file changes go through beads assigned to workers. Implement orch_output banner, spawn/idle/zombie log scenarios
description:
### Summary

Implement the 5 new @wip scenarios in orch_output.feature covering the mode banner, spawn log, idle log, and zombie log output.

### New step patterns needed

1. `the orchestrator ticks in dry-run mode` — run tick with dry-run flag, capture full output including banner
2. `the orchestrator ticks in confirmed mode` — run tick with confirmed flag, capture full output
3. `zombie sessions:` (with table) — set up zombie session data with columns: bead, reason

### Harness functions needed

- `orch-tick-dry-run!` — run tick in dry-run mode, capture full output (banner + summary + logs)
- `orch-tick-confirmed!` — run tick in confirmed mode, capture full output
- `set-zombie-sessions-from-table [headers rows]` — set up zombies from table data

### Notes

- The banner is produced by `orch_runner_io.clj` (IO layer). The harness will need to either call into the IO layer with stdout capture, or replicate the banner format in a pure function that can be tested.
- Spawn/idle/zombie log formatting is already pure in `orch_runner.clj` (format-spawn-log, format-idle-log, format-zombie-log). The harness just needs to compose the full output: banner + debug output + log lines.

### Acceptance criteria

- `bb spec` — all green
- `bb features` — all 13 orch_output scenarios pass (remove @wip from the 5 new ones)
- Banner, spawn log, idle log, and zombie log all verified with line matching

title: Prevent duplicate iteration-complete notifications — guard against race condition

comments.author: micahmartin

The first worker to mark it complete wins; others see it's already done and stay quiet.

title: Optimize orchestrator: reduce zombie detection overhead
description:
The orchestrator is timing out (300s) because zombie detection is expensive — it calls 'bd show <id>' for every active session label to check if the bead is closed. With multiple projects and sessions this blows the time budget before any workers get spawned.

Fix options:
1. Make zombie detection optional or lazy — skip it if sessions_list shows no project: labeled sessions
2. Batch the bead status checks (bd list instead of individual bd show calls)
3. Move zombie detection to a separate lightweight pass
4. Simplify criteria — only check session status (completed/failed/stopped), skip the bead-closed check

Goal: orchestrator should complete in under 60s even with many active projects.

Implement orch_output.feature — step patterns, generator, and harness
description:
### Summary

Implement the step patterns, generator registry entries, and harness functions needed to make `features/orch_output.feature` scenarios executable.

### New step patterns needed

1. `configured projects:` (with table) — sets up projects with columns: slug, status, priority, max-workers, active-iteration, active-workers. Acts as upsert.
2. `project "X" has beads:` (with table) — sets up beads with columns: id, title, status
3. `the output contains lines matching` (with table) — asserts output lines are present (substring match per row)
4. `the output does not contain` (with table) — asserts text is NOT in output
5. `the output has "X" before "Y"` — asserts ordering in output

### Harness functions needed

- `configure-project [slug attrs]` — upsert project into registry and configs
- `set-project-beads [slug beads]` — set beads with id, title, status for a project
- `orch-tick-with-output!` — run tick and capture formatted output
- `tick-output` — accessor for captured output
- `output-contains-line? [line]` — substring match against output lines
- `output-contains? [text]` — substring anywhere in output
- `output-has-before? [a b]` — ordering check

### Implementation changes

- Update `format-debug-output` in `orch.clj` to include ready beads (currently only in-progress/blocked)
- Add bead title to output lines with 20-char truncation
- Run with NO_COLOR for testable output

### Acceptance criteria

- `bb spec` — all green
- `bb features` — all orch_output scenarios pass (remove @wip tags)
- All 8 orch_output scenarios executable

title: Implement worker_session_tracking WIP scenarios — duplicate spawning and missing bead cleanup
description:
### Summary

Implement the 2 @wip scenarios in worker_session_tracking.feature.

### Scenarios

1. **Prevent duplicate spawning when session already active** — When a bead already has an active worker session, the orchestrator should not spawn another worker for it.

2. **Session with missing bead data is marked for cleanup** — When a session references a bead that no longer exists, the session should be flagged for cleanup.

### New step patterns needed

1. \`a session "X" is already active\` — set up an existing active session
2. \`the orchestrator considers spawning for bead "X"\` — trigger spawn consideration for a specific bead
3. \`spawning should be prevented with reason "X"\` — assert spawn was blocked with reason
4. \`no bead exists with id "X"\` — set up state where bead is missing
5. \`checking session validity\` — trigger session validity check
6. \`the session should be flagged for cleanup\` — assert cleanup flag

### Acceptance criteria

- \`bb spec\` — all green
- \`bb features\` — both worker_session_tracking WIP scenarios pass (remove @wip)

title: Replace LLM orchestrator with shell script for true fire-and-forget spawning
description:
The LLM agent orchestrator times out because it can't truly fire-and-forget — it waits for tool call responses. Replace it with a shell script that:

1. Calls braids orch-tick to get spawn decisions
2. For each spawn, calls openclaw agent --agent <id> --message <task> --session-id <uuid> --deliver &
3. Exits immediately after spawning all workers

This eliminates LLM reasoning overhead and provides true fire-and-forget spawning with no timeouts.

Update the cron job to run the shell script instead of the agent turn.

acceptance_criteria: Workers reliably terminate after completing or failing a bead. No more zombie sessions lingering. Shorter default timeouts, worker instructions updated to exit cleanly, orchestrator spawn parameters optimized.

Move it back to spec/braids/gherkin_spec.clj. Also: remove spec-gherkin from :paths in bb.edn, remove the test:gherkin and test:all tasks (they become redundant), and delete the empty spec-gherkin/ directory. title: Log full spawn command in orchestrator output
description:
Add 'spawn cmd: openclaw ...' line to orchestrator output for each worker spawned.

CRITICAL DESIGN CONSTRAINT: The code that generates the logged command string MUST be the same code (build-worker-args) used to actually spawn the worker. No duplicate or parallel command generation — build-worker-args must be called from format-spawn-log, and its output is what gets logged. This ensures the test proves the real command.

Changes required:
1. Revert build-worker-args in src/braids/orch_runner.clj back to 'openclaw agent' (not 'openclaw cron add' — that was a wrong direction from braids-halj/braids-d005). The correct form is:
   openclaw agent --message <task> --session-id braids-<bead>-worker --thinking <level> --timeout <seconds> [--agent <id>]

2. Update format-spawn-log to accept config, call (build-worker-args config spawn) per spawn entry, and append a 'spawn cmd: openclaw <joined-args>' line for each spawn. The joined args must quote/escape the --message value since it contains spaces and newlines (replace with a short placeholder like '<task>' in the log line for readability, but all other flags must appear verbatim).

3. Update call sites of format-spawn-log in orch_runner_io.clj to pass config.

4. Implement the new step definition 'Then the output contains a line matching <regex>' in the feature harness.

5. The new scenario in features/orch_output.feature (already added) must pass:
   Then the output contains a line matching
     'spawn cmd: openclaw agent --message .+ --session-id braids-alpha-aa1-worker --thinking high --timeout 1800 --agent scrapper'

6. Ensure all existing specs still pass. Write deliverable to .braids/iterations/013/XXXX-log-spawn-command.md.
